question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: '] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that DriverManager.getConnection() opens a real database connection (not just a ping) to cause the application to authenticate against the database on every retry iteration, generating excessive authentication log entries that fill the database's audit log?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: '] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the jdbcUrl to include a JDBC driver-specific option that enables query logging to a file path they control (e.g., logFile=/tmp/attacker.log), writing sensitive data to an attacker-accessible location?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: '] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the System.nanoTime() deadline calculation at line 25 to cause a long-running startup probe by providing a jdbcUrl that consistently returns connections that fail isValid() but do not throw exceptions, consuming the full timeout duration?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: '] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the jdbcUrl to specify a JDBC driver that supports loading configuration from a remote URL (e.g., jdbc:mariadb://host/db?credentialType=AWS_IAM), causing the application to make unexpected outbound HTTP requests to attacker-controlled endpoints?
